Title: Salesman charged with CBT for misappropriating RM500,000 jewellery for gambling
Article:
BATU PAHAT: A salesman at a factory producing pure gold was charged in the magistrate's court here today with 35 counts of criminal breach of trust (CBT) for misappropriating jewellery worth more than RM500,000.
Teng Wei Kiang, 33, pleaded not guilty after all charges under Section 408 of the Penal Code were read before magistrate Arun Noval Dass.
The accused was alleged to have misappropriated necklaces, bracelets and rings worth about RM524,000 for his own use between Oct 20 last year and April 14 this year.
The offences were allegedly committed at several pawn shops in this district and in Kluang.
Investigations found that the accused, who had been entrusted to sell newly produced gold to various shops, instead pawned the items to obtain money for gambling.
Police also found that a total of 1.4kg of gold entrusted to the accused for sale resulted in losses exceeding RM700,000 based on current gold prices.
The offence carries a maximum penalty of 14 years' jail, whipping and a fine.
Inspector Aizuddin Sulaiman conducted the prosecution, while lawyer Moganesya Nagendran represented the accused.
The court allowed bail at RM65,000 for all charges and ordered the accused to report to the nearest police station once a month, refrain from approaching prosecution witnesses, and surrender his passport immediately.
The case was fixed for mention again on June 25 for document submission.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: T7 Global's net profit rises 2.8pct in Q1
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: T7 Global Bhd 's net profit gained 2.8 per cent to RM7.24 million in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 from RM7.05 million a year ago lifted by the industrial solutions segment.
The company logged a revenue of RM184.56 million in the quarter under review, up 32.9 per cent year-on-year from RM138.88 million in the same period last year.
In a statement, the company said that its  industrial solutions segment delivered a significant revenue growth of 142 per cent to RM89.2 million.
The energy segment in turn recorded a commendable revenue of RM95.3 million and remained as the company's larger revenue contributor, having accounted for 51.6 per cent of the total revenue.
T7 global executive director Tan Kay Zhuin said the company kicked off the year with a good set of financial performance supported by significant growth in the industrial solutions segment.
"While the energy-related activities experienced a seasonal slowdown during the first quarter, we expect the segment to recover and resume stronger contributions to the group in the coming quarters, supported by ongoing projects within our order book.
"Looking ahead, we remain focused on disciplined execution of our secured contracts while being selective in tendering the projects across both segments to ensure sustainable long-term value creation for our stakeholders," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysian man charged with murder of Singaporean woman
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A Malaysian man was charged in Singapore on Tuesday with the murder of a woman at a Housing and Development Board (HDB) block in the republic's Choa Chu Kang district.
Mohamad Faiz Umar, 22, was accused of murdering Chua Bee Ting, 21,  inside a lift at Block 248 Choa Chu Kang Avenue 2 on May 26.
The incident is believed to have occurred between 8.53pm and 9.22pm, with preliminary investigations indicating that the victim had been stabbed.
Police said the suspect and victim were known to each other, although the nature of their relationship was not disclosed in court documents.
According to the Straits Times, authorities were alerted to the incident at about 8.55pm.
Upon arrival, police found Chua motionless at the lift lobby on the 12th floor. She was pronounced dead at the scene by a paramedic from the Singapore Civil Defence Force (SCDF).
The case has been adjourned to June 18.
The charge follows an incident where police and the SCDF responded to a call for assistance at the residential block.
According to witness accounts, a loud noise was heard at 9pm. Residents subsequently found the suspect lying on the ground floor under a dustbin after he reportedly fell from the block.
He was conscious and was taken to the National University Hospital.
At the scene, police cordoned off areas on both the ground floor and the 12th floor for investigation.
Several family members of those involved were seen in a state of distress at the void deck and on the 12th floor.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Search for boy swept away in Sungai Bernam enters second day
Article:
KUALA KUBU BARU: Search and rescue operations for a 10-year-old boy feared drowned after being swept away by strong currents at Sungai Bernam near Kampung Sungai Nilam here entered their second day today.
Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said rescuers resumed operations this morning, focusing surface searches within a 3km radius from the point where the victim was believed to have fallen into the river.
He identified the victim as Muhamad Aakif Imran Asrul Nizam, 10.
"The department received an emergency call on the incident at 1.40pm yesterday.
"Firefighters from the Kuala Kubu Baru fire station arrived at the scene at 2.35pm before immediately launching search efforts," he said today.
Mukhlis said the operation involved personnel from the Kuala Kubu Baru and Tanjung Malim fire stations, assisted by the Water Rescue Team (PPDA), K9 unit, and firefighters from the KLIA and Port Klang fire stations.
He said on the first day of operations, rescuers carried out searches within a 1.6km radius from the location where the victim was believed to have been swept away by the currents.
"PPDA divers were deployed to conduct dives at the area believed to be the point where the victim fell into the river before being carried away by strong currents," he said.
Mukhlis said today's operation was also assisted by personnel from the Slim River fire station and several other agencies, including police, the Health Ministry, volunteer fire brigades and Universiti Pendidikan Sultan Idris (UPSI).
"The PPDA and K9 units remain at the scene and search operations are ongoing," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kelantan Sultan donates two Brahman cattle for qurban programme
Article:
KOTA BARU: Sultan of Kelantan Sultan Muhammad V has donated two Brahman cattle weighing nearly one tonne each for the Jama'ie Qurban Programme today.
Held at the Sultan Ismail Petra Islamic Complex in Panji, the programme was organised by the Kelantan Islamic Religious and Malay Customs Council (MAIK).
MAIK deputy president Datuk Mohd Anis Hussein said the two cattle were among 55 slaughtered today in conjunction with Aidiladha.
"The meat will be distributed to 1,400 recipients through mosques in the Kota Baru district and surrounding areas," he told reporters.
He said the programme was part of MAIK's efforts to enliven Islamic teachings and strengthen community ties through the distribution of sacrificial meat to those in need.
"This programme is an annual event, and every year MAIK selects different recipients to receive the meat," he added.
MAIK expressed appreciation and gratitude to all contributors, including government agencies, statutory bodies and private donors, for their continued support in ensuring the success of this year's qurban programme.
Also present were Tengku Mahkota Kelantan Tengku Muhammad Fakhry Petra and Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: #NSTviral: Viral dashcam video shows motorcycle hitting stopped SUV in emergency lane [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A viral dashcam video showing a motorcycle crashing into an SUV along an emergency lane has raised concerns over highway safety and visibility on high-speed roads.
The 28-second clip, widely circulated on social media, shows a motorcycle travelling along the emergency lane before crashing into an SUV believed to have been stationary.
Based on the footage, the SUV's brake lights and hazard lights did not appear to be activated, while no warning triangle or safety cones were seen placed behind the vehicle, prompting debate over whether adequate safety precautions had been taken.
The exact location, timing and circumstances of the incident remain unclear, and it is not known whether authorities have taken any action.
The video has sparked discussion over emergency lane usage and roadside safety protocols, particularly the importance of hazard signals and warning devices to alert approaching motorists.
Social media users expressed concern over road safety awareness and driver responsibility.
"Where are the cones?" user @Husni Aku asked.
"Didn't the rider see the stopped vehicle?" user @Ahmad Nazmi questioned.
"Be careful when driving," user @Jas May commented.
"The car owner is at fault. The owner should be fined for not placing cones or a warning triangle 50 metres away and also for not switching on the hazard lights," user @Muhammad Farhan wrote.
"From a distance, the white car could already be seen. I don't know how this could have happened. Maybe it was just fate," user @GorgeousReindeer4366 commented.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hamzah accused of plotting Bersatu takeover, allegedly with Pas, Umno backing
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin has been accused of using Pas and Umno as political leverage in an alleged attempt to split Bersatu and wrest control of the party from Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin.
Former private secretary to Muhyiddin, Datuk Dr Marzuki Mohamad, claimed the move was part of a plan to oust the Bersatu president and pave the way for Hamzah to become acting president.
Marzuki alleged that Hamzah, formerly the party's deputy president, avoided contesting the presidency against Muhyiddin because support for the Pagoh member of parliament remained strong.
Instead, he claimed Hamzah persuaded Muhyiddin to pursue a party transition plan under which he would become deputy president.
Marzuki said Bersatu's constitution stipulates that the deputy president assumes the role of acting president if the president steps down.
"Become deputy president uncontested, then pressure Muhyiddin to resign. After Muhyiddin resigns, he (Hamzah) becomes acting president of Bersatu.
"With the support of Pas and Perikatan Nasional (PN) component parties, he would be appointed chairman of PN, all without having to contest," he said in a statement today.
On Feb 13, Berita Harian reported that Hamzah had been expelled from Bersatu with immediate effect for allegedly violating Clause 9.1.4 following a decision by the party's disciplinary board in accordance with Clause 22.5 of the party constitution.
Marzuki said Hamzah originally held no significant position in Bersatu until Muhyiddin appointed him secretary-general and later endorsed him for the deputy presidency.
He claimed Hamzah won the post uncontested through Muhyiddin's backing.
"Muhyiddin persuaded Datuk Seri Ahmad Faizal Azumu (Bersatu vice-president) to step aside and contest for vice-president instead, allowing Hamzah to win the deputy presidency uncontested.
"The duty of the deputy president under the Bersatu constitution is to assist the president, but Hamzah not only failed to help, he openly moved to topple the president soon after his appointment was confirmed," he said.
Marzuki further alleged that Hamzah sought to pressure Muhyiddin into stepping down by influencing Bersatu division chiefs through a signature collection campaign.
"It is easier to 'influence' 120 Bersatu division chiefs than to secure the support of thousands of delegates across all divisions during party elections," he said.
Bersatu-Pas-Umno cooperation
Marzuki also alleged that Hamzah claimed to have the backing of Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang in efforts to oust Muhyiddin.
He further claimed Hamzah had been working on cooperation between Bersatu, Pas and Umno to form a government on the condition that Muhyiddin be removed before such cooperation could proceed.
Marzuki alleged the condition was set by Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, although he said several senior Umno leaders later denied this.
"There was no such condition. In fact, they told me Umno does not trust Hamzah. That is why all his attempts at a backdoor takeover, from Dubai to Bangkok, failed.
"Additionally, a former pro-Hamzah Bersatu MP once uploaded photos on Facebook of a meeting involving Abdul Hadi, Hamzah and several Bersatu and Pas MPs at the 'White House' (Hamzah's office in Bukit Tunku).
"He spread the narrative that this was where Abdul Hadi endorsed Hamzah, and I personally raised this matter with a senior Pas leader. He denied Abdul Hadi's involvement in Hamzah's movement," he said.
Berita Harian is seeking Hamzah's response regarding the allegations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Not everybody is a loser from the Iran war. Just ask Brunei
Article:
ARGENTINA, Brunei and Gabon are not three countries that would appear to have much in common. But they are in a small group of energy producers that are big winners from the war against Iran.
The focus from the Iran conflict has largely been on the increasing cost the world is having to pay through higher crude oil and natural gas prices resulting from the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz.
Rising inflation, disrupted supply chains and the mounting risk of shortages of refined products, fertilisers and materials to process metal ores mean that ultimately every country is paying a price.
But there are countries where higher prices for crude oil, refined products and liquefied natural gas (LNG) are providing huge benefits that, so far, outweigh the cost of rising inflation.
Brunei, a sultanate on the Southeast Asian island of Borneo with a population of just under 500,000, is a good example.
It has increased exports of crude oil, refined products and LNG since the war started on Feb 28 in order to capture the high prices on offer in Asia, the region most affected by the strait's closure.
Brunei's exports of crude oil were 2.74 million barrels in April, the most in three months and up 51 per cent from the 1.81 million barrels in April 2025, according to data compiled by commodity analysts Kpler.
Kpler estimates Brunei's shipments of refined products will hit 4.16 million barrels in May, the most since July last year, with Australia, the world's biggest importer of diesel, the top destination.
Brunei's exports of LNG are expected to reach 330,000 metric tons in May, down from 370,000 tonnes in April but well above the 280,000 tonnes from May 2025.
Not only has Brunei increased export volumes, it will be enjoying far higher prices, especially for refined products.
Jet fuel prices in Singapore
ended at US$139.18 a barrel on Tuesday, prior to Wednesday's public holiday, and while they have retreated from the record high of US$242.06 on March 30, they are still 49 per cent higher than before the war.
[[nid:1450292]]
The Singapore price of gasoil, the building block for diesel, ended at US$138.68 a barrel on Tuesday, up 52 per cent since the start of the conflict, with both middle distillates outpacing the 30 per cent rise in benchmark Brent crude oil futures , which ended at US$94.29 on Wednesday.
There are also bigger exporters that are benefiting from the loss of at least 10 per cent of global crude oil and about one-fifth of LNG that is shut-in by the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.
Although the strait passes through its territory, Oman's exports are outside the narrow waterway and have been largely unaffected by the conflict.
Oman is forecast to ship 31.58 million barrels of crude in May, the most since October, while exports of refined products are expected by Kpler to rise to a record high of 17.39 million barrels.
LNG exports from Oman are forecast at 1.0 million tonnes in May, down slightly from April's 1.09 million, but ahead of the 830,000 tonnes from May last year.
African oil producers are also enjoying a boon, especially since much of their crude is medium in API gravity - similar to the oil that has been lost to the market due to the war.
Angola is expected to export 31.86 million barrels of crude in May, up from 29.12 million in April, but below the 37.56 million in May, which was the highest in a year.
The southwest African nation also is expected to ship 420,000 tonnes of LNG in May, maintaining a consistent level of exports of the super-chilled fuel.
Gabon's crude oil exports are forecast to reach 7.28 million barrels in May, the most since September, and the mix of buyers has shifted strongly towards Asia since March.
Latin American exporters are also cashing in, with Argentina's crude exports hitting a record high of 9.89 million barrels in May, more than double the volume in February.
Argentina sent only 700,000 barrels of crude to Asia in the last quarter of 2025, but has shipped 7.21 million barrels in the three months from March to May.
Other countries on the winners' list include Guyana, Nigeria, Algeria and Malaysia.
The common thread is that they not only export crude, they also export products or are at least self-sufficient in refined fuels, and some enjoy the added bonus of producing LNG.
In addition, several have large domestic agricultural sectors, which will help insulate them from food inflation caused by rising input costs such as fertiliser and diesel.
* The writer is from Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: P-hailing rider claims trial to issuing death threat against shop assistant
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A p-hailing rider pleaded not guilty at the magistrate's court today to criminally intimidating a hairdressing shop assistant with a death threat last week.
A. Vinodvarma, 36, entered the plea after the charge was read before magistrate Farah Nabihah Muhamad Dan.
He was accused of threatening Ashha Begum at 6.20pm at Taman Bamboo, off Jalan Ipoh in Sentul, on May 22.
The charge, framed under Section 506 of the Penal Code, carries a maximum two-year jail term, a fine, or both upon conviction.
Deputy public prosecutor Amritpreet Kaur Randhawa did not offer bail as the offence is non-bailable.
However, lawyer Erickka Farrise Amir urged the court to exercise its discretion and grant minimum bail.
"The incident arose from a misunderstanding and did not result in any injuries or property damage. My client cooperated with the police and is not a flight risk.
"He is a bachelor and cares for his ailing mother and disabled father. He also does not have a steady income and is currently unable to work after recently suffering a broken left arm in an accident," he said.
The court granted bail at RM4,000 with two sureties and ordered the accused to report to a police station once a month.
The magistrate also warned the accused not to interfere with the victim or prosecution witness, failing which the bail could be revoked.
The court fixed June 30 for mention.
Police investigations revealed that the incident allegedly stemmed from a dispute involving money spent on a gift during an overseas trip, with the accused allegedly demanding repayment and threatening to kill the victim.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: AI chiefs walk back job apocalypse warnings
Article:
WASHINGTON: The most prominent figures in artificial intelligence (AI) are stepping back from dire predictions about mass unemployment, as the industry faces growing public hostility over AI's promised transformation of the workplace.
Nvidia chief executive Jensen Huang and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, whose comments have stoked anxiety about AI's potential effects on society, are now arguing that doom-laden warnings were overblown or, in some cases, disingenuous.
Speaking to Channel News Asia on Monday, Huang took direct aim at fellow executives who have publicly blamed AI for workforce reductions.
"The narrative that connects AI to job loss, for many of the CEOs that are doing it – it is just too lazy," he said.
"AI has just arrived. How is it possible they're already losing jobs?"
Huang, who has long argued that AI will create as many jobs as it displaces, pushed back against the doom-and-gloom forecasts of some industry insiders, saying that the recent wave of corporate layoffs was not driven by AI.
"How is it possible that AI became productive and useful only six months ago, and they were somehow laying people off two years ago because of AI? It doesn't make any sense," he said.
"It was just a way for them to sound smart, and I really hate that. I think we're scaring people and that's irresponsible," he said.
Last week, British bank Standard Chartered announced plans to axe thousands of jobs by 2030 as artificial intelligence replaces employees in a range of administrative roles.
The tech firm behind social network Snapchat cut 1,000 jobs last month, saying AI is boosting efficiency as it pushes towards profitability.
Altman, meanwhile, offered a mea culpa.
Speaking at the Commonwealth Bank of Australia's Accelerate AI Conference in Sydney, he said rapid AI development would not produce the "jobs apocalypse that some of the companies in our space advocate or talk about" – including his own.
"I thought there would have been more impact on entry-level white-collar jobs being eliminated by now than has actually happened," he told the conference on Tuesday, as reported by The Australian.
"I think I understand more about why that wasn't done – obviously gratefully – but that is an area where my intuitions were just off."
Anthropic boss Dario Amodei has also softened his tone, predicting recently that even if 90 per cent of jobs are automated, the remaining 10 percent would be handled by human workers who would be vastly more productive.
Amodei has long drawn criticism from fellow industry figures who regard him as an AI doomer, even as Anthropic has become a highly successful company, with Huang saying last year he "pretty much disagrees with almost everything he says."
The reversals from rivals Altman and Amodei come as their companies – OpenAI and Anthropic – are expected to embark on high-profile IPOs that will require broad buy-in from investors to succeed.
But earlier doom-laden statements have now come to haunt the AI industry as the public, notably in the United States, voices serious discontent in polling over the disruption that tech companies and political leaders predict from AI.
Most economic institutions, most recently the European Central Bank, say that artificial intelligence has had only minor effects on employment so far.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man's appeal against 12-year sentence for assaulting stepdaughters dismissed
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Court of Appeal has dismissed an unemployed man's appeal against his 12-year jail sentence and 14 strokes of the rotan for sexually assaulting his stepdaughters.
Judge Datuk Azmi Ariffin, who has since been elevated to the Federal Court, wrote in a 22-page judgment that the 34-year-old appellant had betrayed the victims' trust and that his late regret carried no weight.
Azmi, sitting alongside Datuk Meor Hashimi Abdul Hamid and Datuk Seri Mohd Radzi Harun, described sexual crimes as disgusting, despicable, and shameful.
He said the victims, aged 16 and 17, were sexually assaulted whenever their mother, a school teacher, was away from home.
"The trust placed in him by the mother to care for, guide and protect her daughters was completely betrayed.
"He should have been an 'umbrella,' protecting the girls from harm but instead, he violated the absolute trust entrusted to him by their mother.
"The immense trust placed in him as a father figure was completely betrayed by him. His actions were like those of a wolf in sheep's clothing," he said.
Azmi said the appellant's remorse held no weight as his actions left a deep and lasting psychological effect on the girls.
"This dark and horrifying incident leaves an indelible mark on the girls' lives, one that we believe will be difficult to erase from their minds throughout their lifetime," he said.
He said sexual crimes must be treated seriously by society, the government, and enforcement agencies, including the courts, as they affect the dignity, safety, and wellbeing of victims.
"Sexual offences are a growing problem and a serious threat to society. They are not a new phenomenon and have long affected communities regardless of race, culture or religion.
"Children in the country must be protected from all forms of sexual exploitation, as they are often vulnerable and may be targeted by individuals seeking to satisfy their sexual impulses," he said.
Azmi said the severe penalty was intended to curb the alarming rise of sexual crimes involving underaged girls and to prevent such offences from escalating unchecked.
"We hope that this strong and stern message conveyed through the sentence will further reinforce public confidence in the justice system's commitment to protecting the rights and welfare of children.
"We must emphasise that public interest must always take precedence over personal interest, especially in cases of sexual crimes against underaged girls," he said.
The appellant had appealed against the sentence imposed by the Selayang Sessions Court after being found guilty of seven sexual assault charges.
Four were framed under Section 14(a) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act read together with Section 16, and three counts were under Section 377C of the Penal Code.
He was also ordered to undergo counselling in prison and be placed under police surveillance for two years upon release.
The offences were committed at a house in Seri Gombak, Batu Caves, in 2015, 2019, and 2020.
According to the facts of the case, the younger girl initially told her mother about the assault, but her mother did not believe her.
In August 2021, the stepfather chased the younger girl out of the house, after which she contacted her uncle and disclosed the abuse.
She later confided in her sister, who confessed that she too had been abused by the stepfather but remained silent because she was only 14 at the time.
Acting on their uncle's advice, the sisters lodged police reports, leading to the stepfather's arrest.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UOA Group co-founder CS Kong passes away at 85
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: UOA Group co-founder and UOA Development Bhd executive director and managing director (MD) Kong Chong Soon, better known as CS Kong, has passed away at the age of 85.
In a Bursa Malaysia filing today, UOA Development said Kong passed away peacefully on May 27, 2026.
UOA Development chairman Kong Pak Lim said Kong had served the company with "exceptional distinction and integrity", providing steadfast leadership and strategic direction throughout his tenure as managing director.
"His contribution to the growth, governance and long-term success of the company has been significant and enduring," he said in the filing.
"The Board wishes to assure shareholders, customers, employees and business partners that the company's operations and governance remain stable and fully supported by the existing leadership team and management structure," Pak Lim said.
Following Kong's passing, Pak Lim will assume the role and responsibilities of MD on an interim basis until a successor is formally appointed within the next three months.
Pak Lim said further announcements regarding board arrangements will be made in due course.
Kong was appointed director and MD of UOA Development on May 27, 2004, and played a pivotal role in shaping the group into one of Malaysia's established property developers.
Together with Pak Lim, Kong co-founded United Overseas Australia Ltd in 1987 and was instrumental in expanding the group's footprint in Malaysia. Under his leadership, UOA delivered a broad portfolio of residential, commercial and industrial developments, particularly across Kuala Lumpur.
Kong brought more than four decades of experience in construction and property development spanning Malaysia and Singapore.
Earlier in his career, he served as a project adviser to the Harapan Group, overseeing the construction of several major hospitality developments in Singapore during the 1970s and 1980s, including the Le Méridien Singapore, Glass Hotel and Changi Méridien Hotel. The projects carried a combined value exceeding S$866 million.
He also held leadership roles in several listed companies in Malaysia and Singapore, including Raleigh Bhd, Town and City Properties Ltd and Tuan Sing Holdings Ltd.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: CIMB shares rise despite softer quarterly earnings
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: CIMB Group Holdings Bhd shares rose in early trade today despite posting lower earnings in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (1Q 2026).
At 10.51 am, CIMB shares rose two sen to RM7.70 with a total of 5.06 million shares traded.
In a Bursa Malaysia filing, the bank said its net profit fell by 2.9 per cent to RM1.91 billion in 1Q 2026 from RM1.97 billion a year ago, while revenue eased by 1.6 per cent to RM5.41 billion from RM5.49 billion previously.
"The performance reflected steady underlying momentum despite foreign exchange and geopolitical headwinds, supported by disciplined execution of its (six-year business) strategy Forward30," it said.
CIMB said its net profit translated into a return on equity (ROE) of 11 per cent and earnings per share of 17.8 sen.
Meanwhile, Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd views the recent profit-taking as a window for investors to capitalise on an increasingly undemanding valuation.
"The bank remains cautiously optimistic regarding its FY2026 ROE target of 11 per cent to 11.5 per cent, anchored by a robust wholesale pipeline in Malaysia and Singapore, stabilising net interest margins, and strong non-interest income.
It is maintaining a "Buy" call with a target price of RM9.80 per share.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rafizi dismisses talk of Nurul Izzah joining Bersama
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli has denied claims that PKR deputy president Nurul Izzah Anwar will join Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama).
The former Pandan member of parliament described the claim as a baseless rumour.
"That is just an empty rumour.
"She will not join Bersama," he said briefly in a post on X.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Rafizi denies RM8.5mil claim over Bersama takeover
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR:  Bersama co-founder Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli has denied allegations that RM8.5 million was paid to take over the political party, describing the claim as slander.
Rafizi said claims surrounding the alleged payment were false and politically motivated.
He alleged that pro-government cybertroopers were behind the rumours, and accused Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil of amplifying the narrative.
"Claims that I paid RM8.5 million to buy Bersama are slander," he said in a statement.
Rafizi said Bersama founder Tan Gin Theam became emotional when handing over the party to him and fellow co-founder Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad.
"He only asked that we take good care of Bersama and ensure it can champion ordinary Malaysians better than he was previously able to," he said.
Rafizi also said the party had surpassed 20,000 members within two weeks, in line with expectations.
He said Bersama would focus on promoting its "12 Agenda Bersama" framework, which he described as an effort to reform the system rather than continue existing policies with minor improvements.
The former economy minister said the party would launch its headquarters, election machinery and "Kancil" mobile application by the end of June as part of preparations to strengthen grassroots organisation and membership management.
He added that applications for prospective election candidates would also be opened soon.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Maybank posts resilient RM2.48bil Q1 profit amid market volatility
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malayan Banking Bhd (Maybank) delivered a resilient first-quarter performance for the period ended March 31, 2026, posting a net profit of RM2.48 billion despite softer trading income amid volatile market conditions.
While net profit eased 4.2 per cent from RM2.58 billion a year earlier, the country's largest bank by assets said earnings remained supported by stronger net interest margins, prudent cost management and stable asset quality.
In a Bursa Malaysia filing, Maybank reported revenue of RM14.92 billion for the quarter compared with RM16.87 billion a year earlier, mainly reflecting weaker trading and markets-related income.
Non-interest income declined to RM1.99 billion, while net operating income fell to RM7.10 billion from RM7.71 billion previously.
However, Maybank recorded an improvement in net interest margin to 2.14 per cent from 2.04 per cent a year earlier, representing a 10 basis-point year-on-year increase, supported by a lower funding cost mix and a higher current account and savings account (CASA) ratio of 41.1 per cent across its home markets.
Maybank said it aims to build scalable businesses across Islamic finance, regional wealth management, transactions and payments, as well as regional corporate and investment banking.
It will also prioritise strengthening foundational capabilities through sustained investments in technology, data and artificial intelligence, developing a future‑ready workforce, and driving productivity and capital optimisation.
President and group chief executive officer Datuk Seri Khairussaleh Ramli said the group's first-quarter performance underscored the strength and resilience of its diversified franchise, as well as disciplined execution amid volatile market conditions.
He said the bank continued to post stable earnings during the quarter, supported by improved net interest margins, prudent cost management and generally stable asset quality.
"We also saw continued progress across several key businesses, particularly wealth management, investment banking and flow business, reflecting the strength of the group's customer franchise.
"Our balance sheet fundamentals remain sound with strong capital and liquidity buffers, as well as continued CASA strength across our home markets, enabling us to continue supporting individuals, small and medium enterprises and large corporates," he said.
No interim dividend was declared for the quarter.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Drowned man's body found in Cheras river after public tip-off
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have confirmed the discovery of an unidentified male body believed to have drowned in Sungai Kerayong near Jalan Cheras.
Cheras police said they received information from the public around 5.13pm yesterday regarding the discovery of the body.
Police, together with personnel from the Fire and Rescue Department and medical officers, were dispatched to the scene to conduct preliminary investigations.
Initial checks found the victim to be a man believed to be under 30 years old. Police said he was believed to have drowned two to three days earlier.
A physical examination conducted by the forensic unit of the Kuala Lumpur police contingent headquarters found no signs of foul play on the body.
The victim's body was sent to the forensic unit of Hospital Canselor Tuanku Muhriz in Cheras for a post-mortem examination to determine the exact cause of death.
The case has been classified as a sudden death report (SDR).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Matrix Concepts posts record FY26 sales, higher profit, declares 1.25 sen dividend
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Matrix Concepts Holdings Bhd recorded all-time high sales of RM1.51 billion for the financial year ended March 31, 2026 (FY26), driven by strong industrial land sales at its Malaysian Vision Valley City (MVV City) development and strong demand across its core townships.
The sales were 9.2 per cent higher compared to last year's RM1.38 billion, largely supported by industrial land transactions at MVV City, which contributed RM362.6 million in sales during the year.
Matrix Concepts' flagship Sendayan Developments township in Negeri Sembilan remained its largest contributor, generating RM884.6 million in sales as demand continued for its integrated and value-driven developments.
The company's net profit increased 2.5 per cent to RM219.3 million from RM214 million in FY25, while revenue for FY26 rose 18.2 per cent to RM1.36 billion from RM1.15 billion in the previous year, backed by ongoing construction progress, contributions from new projects and then expansion of its industrial property segment.
Matrix Concepts chairman Datuk Haji Mohamad Haslah Mohamad Amin said FY26 marked another important milestone for the group as it continued to diversify its earnings base beyond traditional residential developments.
"Our position remains firmly anchored in Negeri Sembilan by the continued success of Sendayan Developments, while MVV City is poised to become the group's next major growth catalyst.
"With an estimated gross development value (GDV) of RM15 billion to be progressively unlocked over the next 12 years, MVV City is expected to drive long-term earnings growth while strengthening our exposure to the industrial and logistics segment," he added.
The development also recorded its maiden industrial property revenue contribution in the fourth quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q4 FY26), marking the beginning of a new earnings pillar for the group.
During FY26, Matrix Concepts launched projects with a combined GDV of nearly RM2 billion, with Sendayan Developments and MVV City accounting for RM915.1 million and RM902.2 million, respectively. Overall take-up rates across developments stood at 77 per cent.
Outside Negeri Sembilan, the group continued to expand its presence in other regions, with Levia Residence in Cheras recording a strong revenue growth of 272.3 per cent year-on-year to RM153.2 million, while Bandar Seri Impian in Johor posted a 94.5 per cent increase in revenue to RM70.3 million.
The acquisition of Horizon L&L Group in August 2025 also contributed RM79.2 million in revenue during FY26, strengthening Matrix Concepts' footprint in the Sepang and Banting corridors.
Overseas, the completed M333 St Kilda project in Melbourne, Australia, contributed RM71.6 million in revenue during FY26.
As at March 31, 2026, unbilled sales stood at RM1.51 billion, providing earnings visibility over the next 15 to 18 months.
The group declared a dividend of 1.25 sen for FY26, payable on July 9.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: AmBank raises dividend payout on higher earnings for FY26
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: AMMB Holdings Bhd's (AmBank) net profit rose five per cent to RM2.1 billion in the financial year ended March 31, 2026 (FY26) from RM2 billion a year ago.
The improved performance was attributed to a higher net interest income which climbed 4.5 per cent to RM3.8 billion as well as a 5.1 per cent rise in non-interest income to RM1.4 billion. These were driven by higher securities trading gains from group treasury and markets and a resilient performance in group wealth management.
Revenue for FY26 rose to RM5.16 billion from RM4.92 billion a year ago.
Total gross loans, advances and financing increased 5.6 per cent in FY26 to RM146.7 billion driven by strong growth from wholesale banking and business banking.
Gross impaired loss ratio was slightly higher year-on-year (YoY) at 1.59 per cent.
Total customer deposits grew 3.9 per cent YoY to RM147 billion, supported by a 4.8 per cent YoY growth in time deposits to RM94.9 billion, and a 2.1 per cent YoY growth in current account savings account (CASA) to RM52.1 billion.
AmBank said that the increase in customer deposits was broad based in retail banking, business banking as well as in corporate and transaction banking.
The group declared a dividend of 22.5 sen per share, bringing the total dividend for FY26 to 35 sen, an increase of 15.9 per cent YoY. This represents a higher payout ratio of 55 per cent versus 50 per cent in FY25.
For the fourth quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q4 FY26), the bank posted a 1.3 per cent increase in net profit at RM520.5 million from RM513.9 million a year earlier. Revenue rose to RM1.3 billion from RM1.28 billion.
AmBank group chief executive officer Jamie Ling said that the bank's balance sheet remains robust, with strong capital ratios and ample liquidity.
"Malaysia's economic indicators for the first quarter remained strong. The resolution of the Middle East conflict remains uncertain, with a prolonged conflict scenario posing risk to global economic growth due to higher energy and commodity prices as well as supply chain disruptions.
"We are prepared for more challenging operating conditions ahead. However, we start from a position of strength and remain responsive to support our customers," he added.
At the midday break, AmBank's share price rose half a sen or 0.76 per cent to RM6.61, valuing the company at RM21.9 billion.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump refiles US$10 billion defamation suit against WSJ over report on Epstein ties
Article:
NEW YORK: President Donald Trump on Wednesday refiled a defamation lawsuit seeking at least US$10 billion in damages against the Wall Street Journal over its reporting on his ties to Jeffrey Epstein, after a judge threw out an earlier version over legal deficiencies.
The lawsuit is one of several Trump has brought in his personal capacity against news organisations, part of what critics say is a wider pressure campaign against the media.
Trump's lawsuit said the Rupert Murdoch-owned newspaper tarnished his reputation with an article describing a birthday card to deceased sex offender Jeffrey Epstein as bearing Trump's signature.
Trump and his lawyers said the card is fake, even after it was released ​by lawmakers investigating Epstein's case.
Trump is seeking at least US$10 billion in damages, according to the amended lawsuit. He had sought the same amount previously.
"At the time of publication, Defendants recklessly disregarded whether the Defamatory Statements were true and/or they purposefully avoided the discovery of the truth," lawyers for Trump wrote in the amended complaint.
⁠The lawsuit filed in Miami federal court names Rupert Murdoch, Dow Jones, News Corp and its CEO Robert Thomson, along with two Wall Street Journal reporters, Khadeeja Safdar and Joseph Palazzolo, as defendants, saying they defamed Trump and caused him to suffer "overwhelming" financial and reputational harm.
Dow Jones has said it has full confidence in the rigour and accuracy of the Journal's reporting and will vigorously defend the lawsuit.
Epstein, the disgraced financier and sex offender, died in a New York jail cell in 2019.
His case generated conspiracy theories that became popular among Trump's base of supporters who believed the government was covering up Epstein's ties to the rich and powerful.
Trump has said he parted ways with Epstein before the financier's legal troubles became public in 2006.
US District Court Judge Darrin P. Gayles, an appointee of former President Barack Obama, threw out Trump's first complaint in April.
The judge found Trump had not met the "actual malice" legal standard for public figures in defamation cases, which requires evidence that a defendant published a statement that they knew or should have known was false.
Trump has also filed defamation and other lawsuits against other media organisations, including the New York Times, the BBC and Iowa's Des Moines Register.
Those outlets have denied wrongdoing and are fighting the cases in court.
Trump's administration has acted to restrict press access to government agencies and threatened to use regulatory powers against critical outlets, drawing legal challenges by media organisations.
The White House has described Trump as the most open and accessible US ⁠president ever, saying his administration has broadened press access in unprecedented ways.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pas Syura Council to decide on ties with Bersatu, says Mohd Amar
Article:
KOTA BARU: Any decision regarding Pas' relationship with Bersatu will be determined by the party's Majlis Syura Ulama, said Pas vice-president Datuk Mohd Amar Nik Abdullah.
He said the party was still waiting for a full report from the Perikatan Nasional (PN) coalition leadership before any move could be made.
"At the moment, we are waiting for a report from the coalition leaders.
"Once the report is completed, it will be presented to the Majlis Syura. We have to wait for the report to be finalised first," the Panchor assemblyman said at the Kelantan Islamic Religious and Malay Customs Council Hari Raya Aidiladha sacrificial programme here today.
Amar said the Majlis Syura would study the report in detail before making any decision concerning Pas' ties with Bersatu and the future direction of cooperation within PN.
"The decision will only be made after the report has been scrutinised by the Majlis Syura," he said.
His remarks came amid speculation surrounding the relationship between PN and Bersatu following reports that Pas had held an emergency meeting to discuss matters involving the coalition.
On May 24, Pas held an
emergency meeting
to discuss issues concerning PN, including the party's future with its component partner, Bersatu.
The meeting, chaired by Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang in Kuala Terengganu, involved the party's top leadership, including the Pas Political Bureau and Central Committee.
Tensions between Pas and Bersatu resurfaced after Hadi reportedly said Pas would
re-evaluate its cooperation
with Bersatu following several actions by the party led by Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin, which were seen as violating the spirit of camaraderie within the PN coalition.
The Marang member of parliament was reported to have said the decision was made following a series of actions by Bersatu deemed "unpleasant" by his party.
In response, Bersatu information chief Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz reportedly said the party would raise matters concerning its relationship and cooperation with Pas for in-depth discussion during its Political Bureau and Supreme Council meetings soon.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM762k 'mushroom' drug haul found in plastic bottles, Kelantan house
Article:
KOTA BARU: The General Operations Force (GOF) seized mushroom-based drugs worth RM762,000 following the arrest of a 32-year-old man in Kok Lanas on May 18.
The suspect was detained around 6pm following surveillance and intelligence operations carried out by the GOF Seventh Battalion intelligence branch in the area.
GOF Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said personnel conducting surveillance spotted a man by the roadside with his motorcycle.
"During inspection, the suspect handed over a plastic bag containing five transparent plastic bottles filled with liquid believed to contain mushroom-based drugs.
"Following preliminary questioning, police were led to a second location at a house in Kampung Wakaf Stan.
"Further inspection uncovered 50.4 litres of liquid believed to be mushroom drugs and a mobile phone," he said in a statement today.
Ahmad Radzi said the suspect and seized items had been handed over to the Kota Baru police headquarters for further action.
"The total value of the seizure is estimated at RM762,000 and the case is being investigated under Section 12(2) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Eversendai bags RM400mil new jobs in Singapore and India
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Eversendai Corp Bhd has secured four new contracts worth a combined RM400 million in Singapore and India, further strengthening its order book and regional presence.
In Singapore, the structural steel specialist was awarded a project by Kajima Overseas Asia Singapore Pte Ltd for the Toa Payoh Integrated Development. The project covers the construction of a polyclinic, library, sports facilities, stadium, town park, and basement levels.
In India, the group secured a major contract in Amaravati, Andhra Pradesh, for an integrated office building aimed at establishing a centralised administrative hub.
This 40-storey tower, part of the Secretariat Towers development, is designed to accommodate state-level heads of department offices, Eversendai said in a statement.
The company said the building incorporates advanced engineering features, including column-free interiors to maximise usable space.
It also utilises a central core and external diagrid structural system to enhance efficiency and architectural design, supported by high-precision beam connections. The use of composite metal decking with steel framing is expected to speed up construction and reduce overall structural load.
Expanding its footprint in Mumbai, Eversendai also won a contract from Larsen & Toubro Ltd for structural steel long-span works of the Prestige Tower Y development. Its scope includes structural steel works, engineering, connection design, and the preparation of detailed shop drawings.
Separately, the group was appointed by The New Era Agencies Private Ltd to deliver a high-rise residential building known as Dhuleva One 50 at Malabar Hill, Mumbai, on a cost-plus contract basis.
Eversendai said these project awards underscore its capability in delivering large-scale, high-value engineering projects across multiple markets, supported by its technical expertise and regional execution teams.
Group executive chairman Tan Sri A. K. Nathan, executive chairman, said Eversendai remains confident of overcoming current challenges, supported by several potential project awards expected within the coming months.
"During these challenging times, the confidence and continued support and trust shown by our longstanding clients, stakeholders, and dedicated employees reaffirm the group's market positioning, underlying strength, and operational resilience," he said.
Nathan said while certain external market circumstances may be beyond our control, our focus on executing with excellence and our commitment to rebuilding stronger remain entirely unwavering," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: LSH Capital accepts RAC land offer, expands property pipeline
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Lim Seong Hai Capital Bhd (LSH Capital) has accepted a Letter of Offer from Railway Assets Corp (RAC) to develop two plots of land in Selangor, marking a further expansion of its property development pipeline.
The proposed development covers about 7.04 hectares of RAC-owned land located in Pekan Country Heights, Selangor.
In a Bursa Malaysia filing, the construction and property development group said it accepted the offer on May 26, 2026, following its earlier announcement dated May 15, 2026 on the proposed project.
According to the earlier filing, the offer was made by RAC following a request for proposal (RFP) exercise for the sale and development of the land parcels. The total purchase consideration for the site is RM197.9 million, with the land earmarked for a property development project.
LSH Capital said the transaction remains subject to the execution of definitive agreements and completion of relevant regulatory approvals.
The group added that further announcements will be made to Bursa Malaysia Securities Bhd as and when there are material developments or upon finalisation of the agreement.
LSH Capital's net profit for the quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q2 FY26) fell to RM18.6 million from RM21.58 million a year earlier, while revenue declined 6.7 per cent to RM99.45 million from RM106.6 million.
Despite the softer quarterly earnings, the group continues to build its development pipeline. This includes the LSH Bund project in Jalan Pahang with a gross development value (GDV) of RM500 million, and a mixed development in Setapak, with a GDV of RM403.9 million.
It also has the Jalan FR03 upgrading works in Kuantan in collaboration with Knusford Construction Sdn Bhd.
Shares of LSH Capital closed 2.22 per cent lower at RM1.76 in midday trading today, valuing the group at about RM1.48 billion.
The stock has surged roughly 117.28 per cent over the past year from a low of 79 sen about a year ago.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rehabilitation programme participants fund largest qurban at Sepang centre
Article:
SEPANG: The Sepang Reintegration Centre held its largest qurban programme to date this Aidiladha, with 49 participants in the rehabilitation programme contributing from their own wages to fund the slaughter of 10 cattle.
The figure more than doubled from previous years and grew out of the department's Community Service Integration (CSI) programme, which places participants with selected companies as paid employees.
Selangor and Federal Territories deputy director of Prisons Faizal Ahmad said two-thirds of the participants' wages are channelled into savings, with some setting money aside for up to a year to take part in the qurban.
"This is the third consecutive year we have held this programme, and seeing the participants fund it themselves makes it all the more meaningful," he said.
Seven of the 10 cattle were funded directly by the participants.
The meat was distributed to participants, visiting family members and residents of nearby villages.
Among those who took part was Syamsul, 37, who wished to be identified only by his first name.
He saved about RM150 a month over five months for what became the first qurban of his life, something he had initially planned to perform only after his release.
"I was afraid I would not get the chance, so when the opportunity came, I took it," he said.
Another participant, Arif, 27, began saving earlier, setting aside RM900 over 17 months while working at a hotel through the CSI programme.
"I wanted to do this for everyone here, for my fellow participants and the friends I have made over two years.
"I hope the centre continues to grow," he said.
Faizal said community engagement is a core part of the centre's reintegration philosophy and extends beyond the festive season.
He said participants regularly take part in community service activities, including clean-up programmes and assisting at local events, as part of preparations for life after release.
"The goal is to bring them closer to society and give them the opportunity to prepare themselves for when they are free," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Thai court dismisses royal insult case against ex-opposition leader
Article:
BANGKOK: A Thai court on Thursday dismissed a royal defamation case against a former opposition leader over comments he made during the Covid-19 pandemic, in a rare ruling in favour of a prominent government critic.
Thailand's strict lese majeste law protects the royal family from criticism and carries jail terms of up to 15 years per offence.
Thanathorn Juangroongruangkit, former leader of the now-defunct Future Forward Party, was charged over a Facebook Live broadcast in 2021 criticising the government's procurement of Covid-19 vaccines, linked to a company owned by the king.
Bangkok's Criminal Court ruled the comments were aimed at then-prime minister Prayut Chan-O-Cha's vaccine administration and did not amount to royal defamation, according to Thai Lawyers for Human Rights.
"The evidence brought by the plaintiff was not sufficient," the group wrote on social media.
The verdict is a rare success for a major opposition figure, many of whom rights advocates say have been targeted by judicial processes because they are perceived as challenging to the nation's conservative establishment.
At least three former MPs from the opposition Move Forward Party, a successor of Future Forward, have previously been sentenced under the lese majeste law over their political activities.
Another 10 MPs, including the current opposition leader, Natthaphong Ruengpanyawut, are under court investigation over their support for reforming the strict law.
If found guilty, they could face lifetime bans on holding political office and be stripped of their right to vote for 10 years.
The 47-year-old heir to one of Thailand's largest auto parts manufacturers, Thanathorn was banned from holding office in a separate case in 2020.
But he remains active in politics and helped campaign for the People's Party, the latest iteration of Future Forward, in the last election in February.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Border security tightened following fatal Pattani shooting
Article:
KOTA BARU: Kelantan police have tightened security along the Malaysia-Thailand border following a fatal shooting in Pattani, southern Thailand, on May 25.
Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said the situation along the border remained under control despite concerns arising from the attack, which claimed the life of a policeman's wife and injured their newborn child.
"The situation at our border with Thailand is still under control.
"The incident in Pattani is considered isolated, but we have taken additional precautionary measures following the attack.
"We have instructed all personnel on duty along the border to remain vigilant and always be on alert while carrying out their responsibilities," he said.
Yusoff said this to reporters at the Kelantan Islamic Religious and Malay Customs Council Hari Raya Aidiladha sacrificial programme here today.
He said police were continuing close cooperation with other security agencies to ensure border areas remained secure and peaceful.
Surveillance and monitoring activities, including patrols along illegal routes and border hotspots, have also been enhanced following the incident.
"We will continue monitoring developments closely and ensure the safety of the people and the country's borders are always maintained," he said.
In Monday's incident, four gunmen disguised in Muslim women's attire and headscarves reportedly opened fire on a policeman's vehicle outside the Prasanwittayamulniti School in the Yarang district of Pattani at 3.30pm (Thai time).
The victim's wife, who was a teacher at the school and had recently gone on maternity leave, was killed in the attack. Their two-month-old baby girl and the policeman were injured.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nada targets up to 40,000 drug-related arrests this year
Article:
SEPANG: The National Anti-Drug Agency (Nada) has recorded about 15,000 arrests nationwide so far this year and is targeting between 35,000 and 40,000 arrests by the end of 2026.
Its director-general Datuk Ruslin Jusoh said the arrests involved both drug addicts and substance abusers detained under the agency's expanded enforcement powers.
"Our target this year is between 35,000 and 40,000 arrests. As of May, we have already recorded around 15,000 arrests and I am confident we can achieve the target," he said after attending an Aidiladha korban programme at the Sepang Narcotics Addiction Rehabilitation Centre (Puspen) today.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Traffic builds up on major highways nationwide
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Traffic is building up and moving slowly on several major highways nationwide as of late morning today.
The Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) said congestion was detected on the North-South Expressway (Plus) heading north from the Sungai Besi toll plaza to Taman Teknologi Bukit Jalil.
Traffic was also reported to be slow in both directions at the Johor Causeway between Johor Baru and Woodlands.
On the North-South Expressway Central Link (Elite), northbound traffic was moving slowly from Bandar Gamuda Cove to Putrajaya following an accident at Km25.2, which obstructed the emergency lane.
Southbound traffic on the same highway was also congested from Putrajaya to Bandar Gamuda Cove.
Meanwhile, traffic heading east on the Kuala Lumpur-Karak Expressway was moving slowly near the Gombak toll plaza entry.
On the Damansara-Puchong Expressway (LDP), congestion was reported northbound from Puchong Perdana to Puchong Intan, while southbound traffic was slow from SS2 to Taman Megah.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UEC debate must be viewed through national interest lens, says academic
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: The debate over recognition of the Unified Examination Certificate (UEC) should not be assessed solely on academic merit, but must also take into account the broader national interest and education philosophy.
Former dean of Universiti Sains Malaysia's School of Educational Studies, Associate Professor Dr Shaik Abdul Malik Mohamed Ismail, said many UEC graduates are undeniably capable, disciplined and globally competitive, and their achievements should be fairly acknowledged.
He said, however, that the central policy question was whether the higher education system should formally recognise an examination framework independently designed, administered and governed outside the national education ecosystem.
Shaik Abdul Malik said the national examination structure, built on the legacy of the British O-Level and A-Level systems, had evolved into the current Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) and Sijil Tinggi Persekolahan Malaysia (STPM), administered by the Education Ministry through the Malaysian Examinations Syndicate and Malaysian Examinations Council respectively.
"This development was part of a broader nation-building process aimed at strengthening a shared educational identity rooted in the national language, constitutional values and social cohesion," he told the New Straits Times.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim recently said the government
would further refine details
of its decision allowing school leavers from tahfiz institutions and Chinese independent schools (SMPC), including UEC holders, to pursue selected programmes at public universities.
He expressed confidence that the move would be accepted by all parties, as it allows UEC students access to public universities, subject to the requirement of passing Bahasa Malaysia and History in the SPM examination.
Shaik Abdul Malik, who is also a former chairman of the Malaysia Education Deans' Council, said Malaysia had successfully nationalised its assessment system while maintaining academic standards and international benchmarking.
He also drew distinctions between the UEC and other qualification pathways such as the International General Certificate of Secondary Education (IGCSE), International Baccalaureate (IB) and tahfiz education streams.
He said international qualifications such as the IGCSE and IB were governed by global academic bodies, while tahfiz pathways still required students to sit for core national subjects, including Bahasa Melayu and History, ensuring alignment with the national curriculum.
"Entry into public universities and the civil service still generally requires fulfilment of core SPM requirements, particularly credit passes in Bahasa Melayu, reflecting the continuing centrality of the national examination framework," he added.
According to him, concerns surrounding UEC recognition are not solely about language or academic standards, but also about the implications of recognising a parallel examination system developed outside the national framework by Dong Zong.
"Malaysia's education system is designed not only to produce employable graduates but also to foster citizenship, mutual respect and social cohesion through shared exposure to national subjects," he said.
Shaik Abdul Malik said critics feared formal recognition of a separate system could weaken the role of the national education framework as a unifying platform.
While acknowledging Malaysia's vernacular school system as part of the country's multicultural landscape, he said a common national examination framework remained important in ensuring cohesion and shared benchmarks.
He said recent Cabinet decisions and statements by the Education director-general appeared to reaffirm SPM as the core pillar of the national education system.
"In a rapidly changing global environment, the focus should be on strengthening an education system that balances excellence with inclusivity, cohesion and shared national responsibility," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rapid KL deploys extra staff, keeps free buses and alternative trains running as Ampang-Sri Petaling repair works continue
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Rapid KL said alternative train services and free shuttle buses will remain active until the end of today following the derailment near Chan Sow Lin station that disrupted services on the Ampang–Sri Petaling Line earlier this morning.
In a statement today, the rail operator said repair works were still ongoing, while additional personnel had been deployed across affected stations to assist commuters and manage crowd movement.
“Repair works are still actively ongoing,” Rapid KL said, adding that “alternative train services and shuttle buses remain active until the end of operating hours”.
The operator said trains from Sentul Timur would turn back at Pudu every four minutes, while trains from Putra Heights would terminate at Bandar Tasik Selatan at six-minute intervals.
It also activated several shuttle train services between affected stations, including Bandar Tasik Selatan and Cheras, Ampang and Maluri, as well as Maluri and Chan Sow Lin.
Free shuttle bus services are also operating between Hang Tuah, Pudu, Chan Sow Lin and Cheras stations, as well as between Hang Tuah and Pandan Jaya via several affected stations, with buses arriving every 15 to 20 minutes depending on traffic conditions.
Passengers were advised to use alternative rail routes or other transport options, including the Putrajaya Line via Sungai Besi station, to reduce travel delays.
“To ensure the safety and smooth movement of passengers, auxiliary police officers, station personnel and Rapid Troopers have been deployed at station platforms and concourse areas to assist commuters,” the statement said.
Rapid KL also urged commuters to plan their journeys earlier and monitor its official social media channels and the MyRapid PULSE app for the latest updates.
Earlier today, Rapid KL confirmed that one of its trains had derailed while passing a railway switch near Chan Sow Lin station. No injuries were reported, and all 25 passengers on board were safely evacuated.

Title: GOF raids in Kelantan uncover drugs, undocumented migrants and RM1.1m timber haul
Article:
KOTA BHARU, May 28 — The General Operations Force (GOF) Southeast Brigade carried out four separate operations involving drugs, illegal immigrants, ketum juice and illegal logging activities in Kelantan, with total seizures amounting to RM1.93 million.
Its commander, SAC Ahmad Radzi Hussain, said the operations were conducted under “Op Taring Wawasan Kelantan” (OTWK) and “Op Bersepadu Khazanah” (OBK), involving cooperation with various enforcement agencies.
He said in the first case on May 18, officers detained a man behaving suspiciously while sitting on a motorcycle by the roadside in front of Politeknik Kota Bharu, before discovering five transparent bottles containing 50,400 millilitres of mushroom drug liquid inside a plastic bag.
“The 32-year-old suspect from Perlis later led officers to a house in Kampung Wakaf Stan here, where additional items, including an iPhone 17 Pro Max, were found, bringing the total seizure value to RM762,000,” he said in a statement today.
Ahmad Radzi said in the second case, 19 Myanmar nationals, comprising 13 men and six women aged between 17 and 40, were detained near Kampung Jeram Perdah, Pasir Mas on May 20.
He said all the suspects were believed to have entered Malaysia through illegal routes.
“On the same day at about 1.30 pm, we crippled ketum processing and distribution activities in two separate operations in Kampung Pulau Pisang and Pulau Pak Amat here.
“The operations led to the seizure of various ketum processing equipment, 623 packets of ketum juice amounting to 623 litres and 268 kilogrammes of ketum leaves, with an estimated seizure value of RM58,930,” he said, adding that the activities were believed to have been ongoing for the past three years.
He said the suspects detained were believed to be working part-time processing ketum for daily wages, with the products sold locally at RM10 per packet.
Meanwhile, in the fourth case, Ahmad Radzi said six individuals were detained after officers raided a timber storage facility in Kampung Lubok Tapah, Pasir Mas at about 3.30pm on May 25 after the owner failed to produce operating licences and machinery permits.
“Further inspections uncovered various wood processing machines, heavy machinery, saw blades, different types of logs and 50,000 planks, with total seizures amounting to RM1.1 million,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Negeri Sembilan DKU says no special meeting on May 29 as matter now before courts
Article:
SEREMBAN, May 28 — A requested special meeting involving the Negeri Sembilan Dewan Keadilan dan Undang (DKU) cannot proceed as the matter is currently before the courts, the body’s secretary said in a letter sighted by Malay Mail.
In the letter dated May 26, DKU secretary Raja Norazli Raja Nordin said the proposed special meeting requested by several Negeri Sembilan royal and customary leaders could not be convened due to ongoing court proceedings.
He cited an ongoing legal action identified as “
Saman Pemula NA-24NCvC-240-05/2026
”, adding that any meeting held on the matter could touch on issues currently being litigated.
“DKU is unable to convene a special meeting as requested in view of the ongoing court proceedings,” he wrote.
Raja Norazli also said the individuals involved could not independently convene or call for a DKU meeting, adding that any purported special meeting scheduled for May 29 would not be recognised as an official DKU meeting.
Accordingly, no special meeting will be held on May 29, 2026,” the letter stated.
The secretary further described the move to call the meeting as “not reasonable” and said it could affect the integrity of the ongoing court proceedings.
He added that the DKU reserved its legal rights over any unilateral actions that could prejudice or interfere with the court process.
The letter was addressed to several Negeri Sembilan royal and customary officeholders and copied to the state Secretary, the state legal adviser, and a legal firm.

Title: Delivery man pleads not guilty to threatening salon assistant in Sentul
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — A delivery man pleaded not guilty in the Magistrates’ Court here today to a charge of criminal intimidation against a hair salon assistant last week.
A. Vinodvarma, 36, is accused of committing the act against Ashha Begum, 24, at Taman Bamboo, Off Jalan Ipoh, Sentul, at 6.20pm on May 22.
He was charged under Section 506 of the Penal Code, which carries a maximum prison sentence of two years, or a fine, or both, upon conviction.
Earlier, deputy public prosecutor Amritpreet Kaur Randhawa did not offer bail to the accused, as the offence falls under the non-bailable category.
However, Vinodvarma’s lawyer, Erickka Farrise Amir, sought bail, saying that her client is the family’s breadwinner and poses no flight risk.
“My client is single and cares for his elderly and infirm parents. Therefore, I request that bail be granted,” she said.
Magistrate Farah Nabihah Muhammad Dan then allowed him to be released on bail of RM4,000 with two sureties, along with an additional condition that he must report to the nearest police station once a month.
The accused was also ordered not to interfere with the prosecution’s witnesses or the victim until the case is concluded. Case mention was set for June 30. — Bernama

Title: Thirteen-year-old girl allegedly beaten by group of students after ‘misunderstanding’, say Kota Kinabalu police
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Kota Kinabalu police are investigating the alleged assault of a 13-year-old girl who was reportedly beaten by five female students in an incident that surfaced after a video went viral on social media.
The teenager suffered bruises to her shoulders, legs and left ear, as well as a minor injury to her knee, according to acting Kota Kinabalu police chief Superintendent Syed Lot Syed Abdul Rahman in a statement today.
Police said they received a report from the victim at about 7.51am on May 23, a day after the incident allegedly took place in the city.
Initial investigations found the girl was allegedly assaulted by five students from several different schools in the Kota Kinabalu area following what police described as a misunderstanding and lingering dissatisfaction among those involved.
Syed Lot said all the students were believed to know one another through a WhatsApp group called “Angel”.
Investigators also believe two of the suspects had brought the victim to the location before the group allegedly surrounded and assaulted her.
“The victim also claimed she was threatened and told not to inform her family members or the police about the incident,” he said.
The assault was reportedly witnessed by several students and members of the public before footage of the incident was recorded and later spread online.
Police are investigating the case under Section 147 of the Penal Code for rioting.
A one-minute video purportedly showing the attack had circulated widely on social media since yesterday, with many users condemning the violence involving schoolgirls.

Title: Submissions for phase four of Bantuan Kasih Johor extended until June 28, says MB
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, May 28 — The Johor state government has extended the period for the submission of Bantuan Kasih Johor (BKJ) Fourth Phase until June 28 to give more time to recipients to obtain the assistance.
Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the move was among the immediate improvements to ensure the submission process was more orderly and easy especially for categories with large numbers of recipients such as the elderly and housewives.
According to him, other improvements included increasing the number of counters according to the ratio of recipients at each location, including the provision of complaint and enquiry counters to facilitate review and payment procedures.
“At least 35 review counters and 35 payment counters will be provided for locations with more than 3,000 recipients, while locations with 2,000 recipients and below will involve 10 review counters and 10 payment counters,” he said in a statement after chairing the Fourth Phase BKJ Coordination Meeting here.
Onn Hafiz said the operating hours would also be extended to 12 hours, especially in the Johor Bahru district, to reduce congestion and provide comfort to recipients, especially the elderly.
A waiting room was also provided at locations with a high number of recipients before the review process was carried out and recipients only needed to bring their identification cards for review purposes before payment was made.
In the meantime, he said representatives of the next of kin were allowed to receive assistance on behalf of recipients who were unable to attend, while the state government would go to the homes of eligible recipients if there were no next of kin.
He said members of Southern Volunteers were also mobilised to assist senior citizens or the Disabled (PwD) and the volunteers will be present throughout the assistance handover process.
In addition, he said appeal applications would be opened starting July 1 to provide an opportunity for individuals who were eligible but not yet listed to review and reapply.
He said his party would take note of every opinion and feedback received and implement immediate steps to streamline the assistance handover process.
In this regard, Onn Hafiz said the state government will continue to improve and refine the implementation process from time to time to ensure smoother delivery of BKJ benefits to recipients. — Bernama

Title: Nine hurt, including suspected migrant smuggler, after GOF car chase ends in crash near Jeli
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Nine men were injured, three of them seriously, after a car believed to be transporting undocumented migrants crashed near Kampung Sungai Rual in Jeli, Kelantan, this morning.
Among those seriously hurt was a 21-year-old Malaysian man suspected of acting as a migrant smuggler,
Berita Harian
reported.
The incident happened at Jalan Bukit Cermin at about 7.30am.
Southeast Brigade General Operations Force commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the remaining victims suffered minor injuries and were treated at Jeli Hospital.
He said a General Operations Force (GOF) team from the Seventh Battalion had spotted a suspicious Proton Saga on the East-West Highway.
“The vehicle was believed to be carrying undocumented migrants and, upon realising it was being followed by authorities, the driver sped off to evade arrest,” he reportedly said to the national daily.
The GOF team pursued the vehicle before it lost control near Kampung Sungai Rual and plunged into a roadside embankment.
Police later arrested the Malaysian driver and eight Bangladeshi men aged between 20 and 40.
Authorities also seized 10 yaba pills believed to contain methamphetamine from the vehicle.
“All those detained were taken to Hospital Jeli for treatment, while some were later brought to the Jeli district police headquarters for further action,” Ahmad Radzi said.
The case is being investigated for offences linked to migrant smuggling and drug possession.

Title: ‘Does not tolerate disrespect’: FamilyMart Malaysia suspends employee over viral soup-throwing row with delivery rider
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — FamilyMart Malaysia has suspended an employee accused of throwing soup at a delivery rider following a dispute captured in a viral social media video, according to FMT.
The convenience store chain said the staff member had been taken off duty pending an internal investigation into the incident, which allegedly took place at its outlet in The Core, Kota Damansara.
“Our team is investigating the incident with urgency and the staff (allegedly) involved has been placed off duty,” FamilyMart Malaysia said in a statement to the media organisation.
“FamilyMart does not tolerate any rude, unsafe or disrespectful behaviour towards anyone at our stores.”
The company also urged the public to be patient while the investigation is ongoing, adding that further action would be taken if necessary.
The incident surfaced after a 23-second video began circulating online today showing a heated exchange between a delivery rider and a FamilyMart employee.
In a post on X, a social media user claiming to be the rider alleged that he had approached the employee to ask about his order before the worker responded rudely and threw soup at him during the confrontation.
It remains unclear what triggered the dispute.

Title: In Sarawak, three men arrested for suspected trespass into house compound in Tudan Phase 3
Article:
MIRI, May 28 — Police arrested three local men aged between 21 and 31 on Monday for suspected trespassing into the compound of a house in Tudan Phase 3 here.
Miri district police chief ACP Mohd Farhan Lee Abdullah said police received a report on Sunday night after the homeowner encountered the suspects at his residence at around 9.30pm.
“In his report, the complainant, who is the homeowner, claimed that he encountered the three suspects within the compound of his house before shouting, causing all of them to flee.
“However, the suspects left behind three sling bags containing identity cards as well as two bicycles, which were later seized as case exhibits,” he said in a media statement today.
Acting on information received, a team from the Criminal Investigation Department (CID) of the Miri District Police Headquarters arrested the three men at Kampung Tudan here.
